title: Geo-Impossible VPN Connections
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_connect" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src
| eval country=Country
| sort user _time
| streamstats current=f last(_time) as prev_time last(Country) as prev_country by user
| eval gap_hrs=round((_time-prev_time)/3600,2)
| where isnotnull(prev_country) AND country!=prev_country AND gap_hrs < 6
| table _time, user, prev_country, country, gap_hrs, src
description: Detects logins from two distant countries faster than plausible travel ‚Äî complements static geo allowlists (UC-17.2.3).
value: Detects logins from two distant countries faster than plausible travel ‚Äî complements static geo allowlists (UC-17.2.3).
implementation: Tune time window (e.g., 4‚Äì8h) and distance (optional `haversine` if lat/long from enriched data). Whitelist mobile users and split tunnel carrier NAT.
visualization: Table (impossible travel), Map (prev vs new), Single value (alerts / day).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user Authentication.src span=1h
| where count > 5
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), GeoIP.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: VPN session connect with `src`, `user`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Tune time window (e.g., 4‚Äì8h) and distance (optional `haversine` if lat/long from enriched data). Whitelist mobile users and split tunnel carrier NAT.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="cisco:asa" action="session_connect" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src
| eval country=Country
| sort user _time
| streamstats current=f last(_time) as prev_time last(Country) as prev_country by user
| eval gap_hrs=round((_time-prev_time)/3600,2)
| where isnotnull(prev_country) AND country!=prev_country AND gap_hrs < 6
| table _time, user, prev_country, country, gap_hrs, src
```

Understanding this SPL

**Geo-Impossible VPN Connections** ‚Äî Detects logins from two distant countries faster than plausible travel ‚Äî complements static geo allowlists (UC-17.2.3).

Documented **Data sources**: VPN session connect with `src`, `user`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), GeoIP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vpn; **sourcetype**: cisco:asa. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=vpn, sourcetype="cisco:asa", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Geo-Impossible VPN Connections**): iplocation src
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **country** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
‚Ä¢ `streamstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **gap_hrs** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(prev_country) AND country!=prev_country AND gap_hrs < 6` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Geo-Impossible VPN Connections**): table _time, user, prev_country, country, gap_hrs, src

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user Authentication.src span=1h
| where count > 5
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Geo-Impossible VPN Connections** ‚Äî Detects logins from two distant countries faster than plausible travel ‚Äî complements static geo allowlists (UC-17.2.3).

Documented **Data sources**: VPN session connect with `src`, `user`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-asa`, `Splunk_TA_paloalto` (GlobalProtect), GeoIP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where count > 5` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (impossible travel), Map (prev vs new), Single value (alerts / day).
grandmaExplanation: We catch logins from two distant countries faster than plausible travel ‚Äî complements static geo allowlists (UC-17.2.3) ‚Äî so unsafe traffic and policy bypasses are caught quickly.
